Patch Set 5:

FWIW list_add overwrites the values in nm_list, so I think only the actual list head (nodemap_head) needs to be initialized. It doesn't hurt though. Although it could be slightly confusing since the nm_lists aren't actually list heads, they are just the list entries, as opposed to nm_ranges which is a list head. 

I noticed that I forgot to update the comments for nodemap_cleanup_iter_cb, and I think it would be easier to read if I rename the data argument to be nodemap_head, so I'll make those changes and update the patch.

parents.subject: LU-5319 ptlrpc: embed highest XID in each request

commit.subject: LU-6409 nodemap: don't destroy nodemaps in cfs_hash_for_each
commit.message:
LU-6409 nodemap: don't destroy nodemaps in cfs_hash_for_each

nodemap_destroy might sleep, but cfs_hash_for_each iterators aren't
allowed to sleep. This patch changes nodemap_cleanup_all to avoid
destroying nodemaps in its cfs_hash_for_each iterator. Instead, the
iterator creats a list of nodemaps, and then all the nodemaps in the
list are destroyed.

Signed-off-by: Kit Westneat <kit.westneat@gmail.com>
Change-Id: I1d16f61ba7739af0494944f6515508b3f0d19ba1
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14275
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andrew Perepechko <andrew.perepechko@seagate.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
